issues.title: Drawing viewer lacks fabric.js annotation layer for field markup
issues.description: The DrawingViewer component has no actual annotation/markup layer using fabric.js or any canvas overlay. Superintendents cannot mark up drawings on iPad. PRODUCTION_ROADMAP P0-3 requires pen, highlighter, text, rectangle, circle, arrow, cloud tools with undo/redo. Currently the viewer is a skeleton without real markup capability.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx, make these specific changes: Install fabric (npm install fabric @types/fabric) and build a real annotation layer. BEFORE: The DrawingViewer component renders a basic PDF view or placeholder with no interactive markup tools. There is no fabric.js canvas overlay, no tool state management, and no annotation persistence. AFTER: The DrawingViewer component renders the PDF using an existing PDF renderer, then overlays a fabric.js Canvas on top. It accepts props for drawingId and projectId. It manages tool state (pen, highlighter, text, rectangle, circle, arrow, cloud) via useState. Each tool configures fabric.js drawing mode accordingly. Annotations serialize to JSON via canvas.toJSON() and save to supabase from('drawing_annotations').upsert(). On mount, it loads existing annotations from supabase for the given drawingId and deserializes them onto the canvas via canvas.loadFromJSON(). Include an undo/redo stack (array of JSON snapshots, max 20 levels). Export a MarkupToolbar sub-component that renders tool buttons as a horizontal bar at the top of the viewer. Include color picker with 8 preset colors (#E74C3C, #F47820, #F5A623, #4EC896, #3B82F6, #8B5CF6, #1A1A2E, #FFFFFF). Use theme tokens from src/styles/theme.ts for all spacing and styling. Add aria-labels to every tool button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Drawings page shows no revision history or version comparison
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 9 requires revision management with auto-increment, previous revision access, and side-by-side comparison. The Drawings page fetches revisions via getDrawingRevisionHistory but does not render a revision timeline or comparison UI. Users cannot see what changed between revisions.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, make these specific changes: Add a revision history panel and version comparison toggle to the drawing detail view. BEFORE: The Drawings page lists drawings in a grid/table but when a drawing is selected, there is no revision timeline, no way to view previous revisions, and no side-by-side or overlay comparison between revisions. The getDrawingRevisionHistory endpoint exists in src/api/endpoints/documents.ts but its data is not rendered. AFTER: When a drawing is selected (detail view or expanded panel), render a 'Revision History' section below the viewer. This section shows a vertical timeline of revisions fetched via getDrawingRevisionHistory(drawingId). Each revision entry shows: revision number (Rev 0, Rev 1, etc.), issued_date formatted as readable date, issued_by name, change_description text, and a 'View This Revision' button. Add a 'Compare Revisions' toggle button that when activated shows two dropdowns to select revision A and revision B, then renders both file_url values in side-by-side iframes or PDF viewers using the existing PdfViewer component from src/components/drawings/PdfViewer.tsx. Mark the current (non-superseded) revision with a green 'Current' badge. Use inline styles with theme tokens. Add aria-label='Revision history' to the timeline container. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Files page lacks drag-and-drop upload and folder navigation
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 9 requires drag-and-drop upload supporting PDF, DWG, DXF with processing indicator. The Files page has an UploadZone component imported but the overall file management UX lacks folder tree navigation, breadcrumb path, and the upload flow does not show per-file progress. The page feels like a flat list rather than a document management system.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Files.tsx, make these specific changes: Implement folder navigation with breadcrumbs and enhance the upload experience with per-file progress. BEFORE: The Files page renders a flat list of files. There is no folder tree sidebar or breadcrumb navigation showing the current path. The UploadZone component exists but there is no per-file upload progress indicator, no processing state for multi-page PDFs, and no drag-and-drop visual feedback zone. AFTER: Add a two-panel layout: left panel (240px width) shows a folder tree built from the folder hierarchy in the file data (use the isFolderRow logic from src/api/endpoints/documents.ts). Each folder is expandable/collapsible with a chevron icon. Clicking a folder filters the right panel to show only its children. Add a breadcrumb bar above the file list showing the current folder path (e.g., 'Project Files > Drawings > Architectural') where each segment is clickable to navigate up. The UploadZone should show a dashed border drop target that highlights blue (#3B82F6 at 10% opacity) on dragover with text 'Drop files here to upload'. When files are being uploaded, show a progress list at the bottom of the page with filename, progress bar (0-100%), and status text. Use theme tokens for all colors and spacing. Add role='navigation' and aria-label='File browser' to the folder tree. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Drawing grid and file list missing keyboard navigation and ARIA roles
issues.description: The drawings grid/list has no ARIA grid role, no roving tabindex, and no keyboard navigation (J/K for up/down, Enter to open). Screen readers cannot identify individual drawing cards or their status. This violates Global Acceptance Standards requiring axe-core zero critical/serious violations and keyboard navigability.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, make these specific changes: Add comprehensive ARIA roles and keyboard navigation to the drawings list. BEFORE: The drawings are rendered as a grid or list of cards/rows with no role='grid' or role='listbox', no aria-label on individual items, no tabIndex management, and no keyboard event handlers. Users cannot navigate drawings with keyboard only. AFTER: Wrap the drawings container in a div with role='grid' and aria-label='Project drawings'. Each drawing card/row gets role='row' with aria-label set to the drawing title and sheet number (e.g., 'A1.0 Floor Plan Level 1'). Add tabIndex={0} to the first drawing and tabIndex={-1} to the rest (roving tabindex pattern). Add an onKeyDown handler on the container that handles: ArrowDown/J to move focus to next drawing, ArrowUp/K to move focus to previous drawing, Enter to open the drawing detail/viewer, and Escape to close any open detail panel. When a discipline filter is active, add an aria-live='polite' region that announces 'Showing X drawings filtered by [discipline]'. Add aria-label to every filter button including the discipline pills. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: No AI coordination conflict detection on drawings
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 9 requires an AI Panel listing detected conflicts with severity across disciplines, and an 'Analyze' button for on-demand analysis. The MODULE_SPECIFICATIONS require AI conflict detection cross-referencing drawings, specs, and submittals. Currently there is zero AI integration on the Drawings page — no conflict panel, no analyze button, no connection to any AI edge function.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, make these specific changes: Add an AI Insights panel that displays coordination conflicts and provides an 'Analyze Drawing' action. BEFORE: The Drawings page has no AI integration. There is no panel showing detected conflicts, no 'Analyze' button, and no call to any AI edge function for conflict detection. The ai-copilot edge function exists but is not connected to the drawings workflow. AFTER: Add a collapsible right sidebar panel (320px width, slides in from right) titled 'AI Insights' with a sparkle icon (from Lucide). The panel has two sections: (1) 'Detected Conflicts' showing a list of conflict cards, each with severity badge (high=red, medium=amber, low=blue), description text, and affected drawing sheet numbers. Initially show a placeholder state: 'Select a drawing and click Analyze to detect coordination conflicts.' (2) An 'Analyze Drawing' button (orange primary style) that when clicked calls supabase.functions.invoke('ai-copilot', { body: { project_id: projectId, message: 'Analyze drawing [sheet number] for coordination conflicts with other disciplines', context: { entity_type: 'drawing', entity_id: selectedDrawingId } } }). Parse the response and display as conflict cards. Show a loading spinner with 'Analyzing...' text during the API call. If the AI service is not configured or returns an error, show 'AI analysis unavailable. Ensure the AI service is configured in project settings.' Use theme tokens for all styling. Add aria-label='AI coordination insights' to the panel. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

invented_features: Drawing annotation layer with fabric.js supporting 7 markup tools, undo/redo, and persistence to Supabase
AI coordination conflict detection panel on Drawings page with on-demand analysis via edge function
Revision history timeline with side-by-side version comparison using PdfViewer
competitive_intel: Procore's drawing viewer supports basic markup but requires a separate PlanGrid subscription for full annotation. Fieldwire excels at task-to-plan linking with Apple Pencil support. Autodesk Construction Cloud has the strongest drawing version comparison with automatic change detection. SiteSync currently has no functional markup or version comparison, putting it behind all three competitors. The P0-3 roadmap item (Drawing Viewer with Markup) is the single most important feature to implement — without it, no superintendent will adopt SiteSync for field work.
summary: The Document Management module is severely underdeveloped at 27/100. The Drawings page exists structurally but lacks the two features that matter most to field users: interactive markup and revision comparison. The Files page is a flat list without folder navigation or meaningful upload UX. There is zero AI integration despite the roadmap and acceptance criteria requiring conflict detection. Accessibility is almost non-existent with no keyboard navigation or ARIA roles on either page. The five issues target the highest-impact gaps: building the fabric.js annotation layer (P0-3 roadmap item), adding revision history with comparison, implementing proper file management UX, fixing accessibility violations, and connecting AI conflict detection. Implementing the drawing viewer markup alone would likely double the module score and unblock superintendent adoption.
